mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in real retail sales (RSAFS) over the prior three months signals weakening personal consumption expenditures, which compresses corporate revenue growth expectations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of observations during periods of negative 3-month RSAFS momentum.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points within a three-month window signals accelerating labor market deterioration that precedes downward earnings revisions and consumer retrenchment, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) and decreases in industrial production (INDPRO) capture a joint contraction in labor and manufacturing capacity that is more damaging to equity risk premia than either signal in isolation, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and INDPRO does not exhibit a negative Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns (IC > -0.03) over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) predict negative S&P 500 returns only when consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) is elevated above 210, because high inflation removes the possibility of monetary offset and amplifies margin compression fears; when inflation is low, production slowdowns are less damaging.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL > 210, positive 5-day S&P 500 returns follow INDPRO declines in more than 50% of instances, or the conditional mean return is not negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) below 2.5% signals aggressive monetary easing in response to financial system stress, which compresses the risk-free discount rate and triggers a short-term policy-relief rally in the S&P 500 over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day S&P 500 returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances when FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%, or the mean return conditional on the trigger is not positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) falling below 1,100 thousand units signals a collapse in residential construction activity and downstream demand for building materials, appliances, and credit, which drags aggregate demand lower and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 1,100, or the Sharpe ratio of the conditional short signal is positive.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of negative real GDP growth (GDPC1) and a rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) confirms a synchronous contraction in output and labor markets that defines recessionary dynamics, generating strongly negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of GDPC1 and UNRATE fails to produce negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of co-movement instances, or the interaction term's t-statistic against forward returns is insignificant (|t| < 2.0).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month declines in industrial production (INDPRO) transmit immediately to earnings expectations for the manufacturing and energy sectors, causing investors to revise near-term profit forecasts downward and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day S&P 500 returns follow month-over-month INDPRO declines in fewer than 52% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
